---
description: Pegasus IA workflow and documentation order
applyTo: "**"
---

# Pegasus IA workflow

Use the smallest safe workflow. Small, punctual, low-risk changes with clear acceptance criteria may use a direct-fix path with MCP memory and verification updates. Broader, ambiguous, architectural, or higher-risk changes use the local SDD flow under `docs/pegasus/`: request, PRD, proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply, verify, and handoff.

The orchestrator only coordinates. Delegate PRD, proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply, verify, and handoff to their matching specialized agents in fresh contexts. Phase agents execute directly and never recursively delegate their phase. If delegation is unavailable, blocked, or fails, stop and report. Outside SDD, delegation is mandatory for 4 or more files of required reading, 2 or more non-trivial implementation files, any tests/builds/installs/external tooling, or complexity beyond small mechanical coordination.

Natural-language product intent should trigger PRD discovery automatically. If the user describes an idea or asks to draft a PRD, do not expose Pegasus internals; call MCP `health` before recovery, recover/search context if healthy, ensure the project exists when recovery reports `project_not_found`, ask one concise round of key product/business questions before editing or finalizing when product decisions are open, never silently decide product scope, draft or refine `docs/pegasus/prd.md` or `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/prd.md`, validate the artifact by reading it back, before any git command first check for `.git` and never run `git diff`, `git status`, or other git validation in non-git workspaces; do not try git first and fall back, tell the user the PRD file path and ask them to review it, call `ensure_change` before recording a new change PRD under `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/` using only `project_id` and `change_id` by default; add `key`, `title`, `status`, or `description` only when needed, and use `kind` only if classification is needed. Never send `type` or both `kind` and `type`, even if equal; never send nested `metadata`, arrays, decisions, questions, or artifact summaries to `ensure_change`, save PRD status/decisions/questions and artifact reference through MCP after `health` succeeds with `record_observation` and `record_artifact`, include a small MCP persistence summary marking `ensure_project`, `ensure_change`, `record_artifact`, and `record_observation` as `succeeded`, `not needed`, or `failed: <reason>`, say the PRD is file-only if required artifact or observation persistence failed, wait for explicit PRD approval before proposal/spec/design/tasks/apply, and do not implement code during PRD flow.

Phase artifacts stay file-based under `docs/pegasus/` or change-scoped `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/` paths. Use MCP for durable memory summaries, task progress, handoffs, observations, and artifact references. Follow `pegasus-sdd-boundaries.instructions.md` for English-by-default operational output and `pegasus-memory.instructions.md` for English durable descriptive prose, exact source preservation, and source artifact-language recording.

If requirements are missing, contradictory, or too broad, stop and clarify or update the docs before editing implementation files.

Prefer small, reviewable work units. Each unit should include its verification evidence and a clear rollback boundary.

Before moving to the next SDD phase, confirm the required prior docs exist and ask for user approval. Proposal requires an approved PRD.

Before proposal drafting, inspect the referenced PRD artifact's Approval table/status. It must say `Approved`; if an approval checkbox exists, it must be checked, and both indicators must agree. Do not treat conversational approval as a substitute for a Draft or inconsistent PRD. Stop and ask for the artifact to be updated and approved first. Every proposal product claim must be traceable to explicit PRD text; never call an unstated detail a preserved PRD assumption. The current change PRD is the only default product-content source, and the canonical managed proposal template/current change placeholder is the only default structure/format source. Do not search, read, inspect, or reuse neighboring or unrelated change artifacts for content, scope, decisions, assumptions, wording, style, or formatting. Consult another change only when the current PRD, active MCP context, or direct user instruction explicitly declares a dependency/relation. When consulted, disclose the reference/change, exact purpose/dependency, and that it was not used as an implicit scope source; never inherit scope, decisions, assumptions, wording, or style implicitly. A material gap is a missing, contradictory, or unverified detail that can alter scope, user-visible behavior, acceptance, risk, or a phase gate. Reconcile every material gap before finalization: resolve it only through explicit reliable current-change evidence or a direct user answer and record that evidence in `Open Decisions / Material Gaps`, or keep a visible unresolved entry there with owner, impact, next step, and needed-by gate. An ambiguous MCP response never resolves a material gap. Classify gaps before writing: for a blocking gap, ask one concise question and stop before writing/finalizing; after it resolves, record its evidence in `Open Decisions / Material Gaps` before proceeding. For a non-blocking gap, keep an unresolved entry in that dedicated proposal section and do not invent a default. Reconcile again after writing and before marker validation or MCP persistence. A new change-scoped proposal must use its actual `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/proposal.md` path in both Pegasus managed markers. The final response must summarize resolved and unresolved gaps and must not claim no open questions while unresolved gaps remain. The final response must contain the exact `MCP persistence summary:` block with six lines for `ensure_project`, `ensure_change`, `record_artifact`, `record_observation`, `record_task_progress`, and `record_handoff`, each as `succeeded`, `not needed`, or `failed: <reason>`, including when MCP is unavailable. If required proposal artifact or observation persistence fails, append `Proposal persistence: file-only — <reason>`.

Before spec drafting, inspect the current change PRD and proposal artifacts. Both must be approved in-file; conversational approval never overrides Draft, Pending, unchecked, or inconsistent artifacts, and all present approval table/status/checkbox indicators must agree. The current change PRD and proposal are the only default product and requirements sources. Use the canonical managed spec template/current placeholder for structure only; do not inspect or reuse neighboring changes for requirements, scenarios, wording, style, or formatting unless the current PRD, active Pegasus Memory context, or direct user instruction explicitly declares a dependency/relation. Disclose every permitted related-change consultation with its reference, purpose, and statement that it was not an implicit scope source. Reconcile every material requirements or acceptance gap before persistence and final response: resolve it only with reliable current-change evidence or a direct user answer, or retain owner, impact, next step, and needed-by gate visibly. An ambiguous Pegasus Memory response never resolves a gap; for a blocking gap, ask one concise question and stop before finalizing. Every normative requirement must trace to approved PRD/proposal evidence or a visible unresolved gap. Preserve existing spec markers and edit only between them. A new change-scoped spec must start with `<!-- pegasus-harness:start path=docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/spec.md ownership=full-file -->` and end with `<!-- pegasus-harness:end path=docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/spec.md -->`; reread exact first/last lines, repair, and reread before Pegasus Memory persistence. After validation, call or attempt `record_task_progress` for phase `spec` before `record_handoff` whenever Pegasus Memory is healthy. For a successfully drafted spec ready for user review, use status `completed` on the first attempt; record the artifact path, `ready for review` / draft complete, open gaps/blockers, and next action `user review/approval` in descriptive fields or notes. The supported status enum is exactly `pending`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`: use `blocked` when blocked, `in_progress` for active work, and `pending` for work not yet started. Never send `ready-for-review` or `completed-as-draft` as a status. Do not return until all six operations have truthful terminal statuses. The final response must include all six `Pegasus Memory persistence summary:` status lines even when Pegasus Memory is unavailable; never invent `succeeded` for an omitted call. `record_artifact` or `record_observation` failure requires `Spec persistence: file-only — <reason>`; if both succeeded but `record_task_progress` or `record_handoff` fails, require `Pegasus Memory persistence incomplete/partial — <failed operation>: <reason>`. Any failed required closure operation forbids a full durable-completion or Pegasus Memory-success claim. Spec stays acceptance-only: requirements, scenarios, edge cases, non-goals, and traceability; no architecture, tasks, or implementation.

For spec work, select one artifact language before writing: an explicit user artifact-language request wins; otherwise use English. Chat, persona, approved-source language, and prior artifacts never infer an override. After exact marker validation and before Pegasus Memory persistence, run a separate language/terminology validation. In Spanish mode, it must scan structural labels: require `Creado:` and `Destino:`; reject `Created:`, `Target:`, and every applicable default-English canonical heading/table label from the canonical spec-template vocabulary. This structural scan permits standardized `GIVEN` / `WHEN` / `THEN`, contractually required canonical enum values such as `Approved` or `Draft`, paths, identifiers, tool/server names, code, source-section references, and established technical terms. For Spanish, translate all human-readable headings, table labels, metadata labels, and prose into neutral, professional Spanish. Repair only affected language blocks, reread the entire artifact, revalidate markers, and rerun the gate. `Language gate: passed` is forbidden while any prohibited English structural label remains. If issues remain, report every exact issue, make no Pegasus Memory persistence call or success claim, and state `Spec persistence: file-only — language validation failed: <exact issues>`. Before the exact Pegasus Memory persistence summary, state `Artifact language: <selected language>` and `Language gate: <passed|blocked: exact unresolved issues>`.

Before design, read the current change's approved in-file PRD, proposal, and spec; conversational approval never overrides any missing, Draft, Pending, unchecked, or inconsistent artifact. Use only those current-change sources by default, and disclose any explicitly related change. The active design is `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/design.md`; root `docs/pegasus/design.md` is its canonical template only. Classify technical context as `existing system with implementation evidence` or the artifact-language-specific Greenfield classification, recording inspected repository code, architecture, and configuration evidence. Identify platform/runtime/framework constraints, existing stack, integration boundaries, persistence, deployment constraints, and other material technical decisions before writing. An approval/source blocker or blocking gap blocks artifact finalization/persistence but allows minimal Pegasus Memory control-state persistence: when healthy, ensure project/change, record blocker observation, `record_task_progress` status `blocked`, then handoff; `record_artifact` is not needed because no artifact was written. Trace decisions to a spec requirement or explicit evidence. Preserve exact change-scoped design markers, reread/repair/revalidate before completed-path persistence, and apply the selected-artifact-language gate. Before persistence reconcile every mentioned material gap against its visible disposition; never ask a close-out question after persistence. Design excludes tasks, work units, and implementation code. Its exact Pegasus Memory summary has six terminal-status lines; use only `pending`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`, record progress before handoff, and use `completed` only when design is ready for review with no blocking gaps.

For design, every individual flow, alternative, affected-area, testing, rollout/rollback, and risk entry requires its own spec-requirement or explicit repository-evidence reference. `sdd-design` returns the complete specialist envelope and owns writing, marker/language/traceability validation, and persistence. After return, the orchestrator validates only envelope completeness, summarizes it without claiming direct artifact validation, and asks for phase approval.

For design, reconcile every material gap and deferred technical choice before marker, language, or Pegasus Memory persistence gates. Each deferred choice must be in a dedicated `Deferred Technical Choices` table with choice/topic, canonical `deferred-non-blocking` status (or translation), owner, impact, next step, needed-by gate, invariant architecture, why non-blocking, and evidence/source. A missing field blocks completed/ready-for-review status; when none exist, state `None` / `Ninguna`, not `TBD`. In Greenfield context without concrete implementation stack, framework, or runtime evidence, `None` / `Ninguna` is invalid: stack/framework/runtime selection is a structured non-blocking deferred choice needed before tasks/apply, while logical components, responsibilities, boundaries, interfaces, and control flow remain independent of it. Record deferred choices and their next gate in task-progress notes and under the exact final-response label `Deferred technical choices:`. English uses `Greenfield / no implementation evidence`; Spanish rejects both `Greenfield/no implementation evidence` and `Greenfield / no implementation evidence`, using `Greenfield / sin evidencia de implementación`. Spanish requires the exact heading `Decisiones y compensaciones` and rejects `Tradeoffs`, `Costos y compromisos`, `Compensaciones`, and `Decisiones y costos y compromisos` as headings. Permit only justified immutable technical exceptions. Persistence-product references must be `Pegasus Memory` or exact server annotation `pegasus-memory-mcp`; reject standalone/generic `MCP`, `Contexto MCP`, `Memoria MCP`, and `Memoria Pegasus`. Allow `MCP` only in explicit protocol discussion such as `protocolo MCP`, or inside the exact server annotation. Repair affected blocks, reread, revalidate markers, and rerun the language gate before persistence. Narrative prose does not replace the exact `Artifact language:`, `Language gate:`, `Deferred technical choices:`, and six-state `Pegasus Memory persistence summary:` structures.

For design language validation, check every `MCP` occurrence independently. Only exact `protocolo MCP` and exact `pegasus-memory-mcp` are allowed; an allowed occurrence never permits a separate standalone `MCP` elsewhere in the artifact.

Before delegating a phase/task, derive identity from change, phase, and task slice, then check MCP task progress and `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/apply-progress.md`. Do not launch duplicate work.

Session preflight establishes review budget and delivery preference, not workload. Before finalizing tasks, `sdd-tasks` estimates authored and generated changed lines, includes tests, and emits the exact forecast fields. After tasks and before apply, the orchestrator consults the user when budget is exceeded, risk is High, chaining is recommended, or a decision is needed. It offers `stacked-to-main`, `feature-branch-chain`, or explicit maintainer-approved `size:exception` and never chooses silently. Apply requires that resolved strategy plus one authorized slice or returns blocked before writing; verification belongs to a distinct fresh-context verifier.

During apply, merge implementation slices, changed files, evidence, blockers, and next action into `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/apply-progress.md`.

When durable workflow state changes, call MCP `health` first. After `health` succeeds, call `ensure_project` before writes when recovery reports `project_not_found`, and call `ensure_change` before change-scoped artifact or observation writes. Then save decisions, observations, task progress, artifact references, and handoffs through MCP. If MCP is unavailable, show exactly: `El pegasus-memory-mcp no se encuentra disponible, si continuamos con eso asi, no se guardara nada de lo que hagamos en memoria persistente`; artifact work may continue, but do not fall back to Markdown memory.

Upgrade/sync updates Pegasus IA configuration surfaces, generated prompts/agents, and Pegasus Memory binary/config references only. It must not reset, delete, recreate, or overwrite the Pegasus Memory database. The only allowed database mutation is an explicit Pegasus Memory schema migration performed by Pegasus Memory itself when that component detects or ships a newer schema version; clean test memory must be explicit test setup, not a sync side effect.

During verify, use fresh context when possible by re-reading PRD, proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply-progress, verify log, and changed files before judging completion. Before any git command, first check for `.git`; in non-git workspaces, never run `git diff`, `git status`, or other git validation and validate artifacts by reading them directly. This is an operational rule, not a runtime guarantee.
